primary_targets: GABAergic modulation; cortisol reduction; thyroid hormone modulation; acetylcholinesterase inhibition
mechanism_text: Ashwagandha withanolides act as positive allosteric modulators of GABA-A receptors, providing anxiolytic effects. Reduces cortisol through HPA axis normalization, inhibits acetylcholinesterase, enhances BDNF and GDNF expression, modulates thyroid hormones (T3, T4), increases DHEA-S, and exhibits neuroprotective effects through antioxidant mechanisms. Promotes axonal and dendritic regeneration in damaged neurons.
cognitive_domains: anxiety reduction, stress resilience, sleep quality, memory, reaction time, testosterone
brain_regions_expected: amygdala, hypothalamus, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, basal ganglia

primary_targets: Serotonergic (5-HT1A agonism); cholinergic enhancement; antioxidant; BDNF upregulation
mechanism_text: Bacopa monnieri bacosides enhance serotonergic neurotransmission through 5-HT1A receptor agonism, increase acetylcholine levels via acetylcholinesterase inhibition, upregulate BDNF and nerve growth factor expression in hippocampus, and reduce cortisol through HPA axis modulation. Enhances dendritic branching and synaptic plasticity in hippocampal CA1 and CA3 regions. Antioxidant effects via superoxide dismutase and glutathione peroxidase upregulation.
cognitive_domains: memory consolidation, learning rate, attention, anxiety reduction, information processing speed
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, amygdala, basal forebrain, temporal cortex

primary_targets: NGF and BDNF upregulation; kappa-opioid receptor agonism; TrkA activation
mechanism_text: Lion's mane hericenones and erinacines cross the blood-brain barrier and stimulate nerve growth factor synthesis and secretion in astrocytes. Erinacine A activates TrkA receptors promoting neurite outgrowth. Hericenones activate kappa-opioid receptors. Promotes hippocampal neurogenesis, enhances myelination through oligodendrocyte differentiation, and reduces neuroinflammation via NF-kB inhibition. Shown to improve cognitive function in mild cognitive impairment.
cognitive_domains: neuroplasticity, memory, mild cognitive impairment, nerve regeneration, depression
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide, cerebellum, basal forebrain, white matter tracts

primary_targets: Glutamate receptor modulation; GABA increase; alpha-wave enhancement; serotonin/dopamine modulation
mechanism_text: L-Theanine crosses the blood-brain barrier and modulates glutamatergic neurotransmission through structural similarity to glutamate, binding weakly to AMPA, kainate, and NMDA receptors. Increases GABA, serotonin, and dopamine levels in brain. Selectively increases alpha-wave oscillatory activity (8-13 Hz) in cortex, producing relaxed alertness without sedation. Enhances BDNF and NGF expression. Synergistic effects with caffeine on attention.
cognitive_domains: relaxation, focused attention, alpha-wave activity, anxiety reduction, sleep quality
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, parietal cortex, occipital cortex, thalamus, hippocampus

primary_targets: Phosphocreatine energy buffer; ATP regeneration in neurons; neuroprotection
mechanism_text: Creatine supplementation increases brain phosphocreatine stores, enhancing the phosphocreatine-creatine kinase energy buffer system in neurons. This accelerates ATP regeneration during high cognitive demand, particularly benefiting prefrontal cortex function during executive tasks. Provides neuroprotection against excitotoxicity and oxidative stress. Effects most pronounced under conditions of sleep deprivation, metabolic stress, or vegetarian diet.
cognitive_domains: working memory under stress, executive function, mental fatigue resistance, sleep deprivation recovery
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, basal ganglia, cortex-wide (metabolic)

primary_targets: Acetylcholine precursor; phosphatidylcholine precursor; growth hormone secretagogue
mechanism_text: Alpha-GPC is the most bioavailable choline source for brain acetylcholine synthesis, crossing the blood-brain barrier to directly supply choline for acetylcholine production in cholinergic neurons. Also provides glycerophosphate for phospholipid membrane synthesis. Increases growth hormone release via cholinergic stimulation of hypothalamic nuclei. Supports myelination through phosphatidylcholine supply.
cognitive_domains: memory formation, attention, learning, neuroprotection, physical power output
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, basal forebrain, prefrontal cortex, hypothalamus, motor cortex

primary_targets: Acetylcholine precursor; phosphatidylcholine precursor; dopamine modulator
mechanism_text: Citicoline provides both choline for acetylcholine synthesis and cytidine which converts to uridine, enhancing neuronal membrane phospholipid synthesis. Increases dopamine receptor density in striatum, enhances norepinephrine and dopamine release in frontal cortex. Reduces phospholipase A2 activity during ischemia, preserving membrane integrity. Promotes BDNF expression.
cognitive_domains: attention, memory, executive function, neuroprotection, visual function
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, striatum, hippocampus, visual cortex, basal forebrain

primary_targets: Reversible acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; NMDA receptor antagonist
mechanism_text: Huperzine A is a potent, selective, and reversible inhibitor of acetylcholinesterase, increasing synaptic acetylcholine levels throughout cortex and hippocampus. Additionally acts as a non-competitive NMDA receptor antagonist, providing neuroprotection against glutamate excitotoxicity. Enhances nerve growth factor signaling and reduces oxidative stress in hippocampal neurons.
cognitive_domains: memory, learning, neuroprotection, attention, Alzheimer's disease symptoms
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide, basal forebrain, entorhinal cortex, amygdala

primary_targets: DAT inhibitor; orexin system activation; histamine increase; norepinephrine increase
mechanism_text: Modafinil inhibits dopamine reuptake through DAT binding, activates orexin/hypocretin neurons in lateral hypothalamus promoting wakefulness, increases histamine release from tuberomammillary nucleus, enhances norepinephrine release in prefrontal cortex, and modulates GABA-glutamate balance. Unlike amphetamines, produces wakefulness without peripheral sympathetic activation. Enhances prefrontal cortical function particularly during sleep deprivation.
cognitive_domains: wakefulness, executive function, working memory, attention, decision making under fatigue
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, lateral hypothalamus, anterior cingulate, thalamus, basal ganglia

primary_targets: BDNF upregulation; TrkB activation; melanocortin system modulation; NMDA enhancement
mechanism_text: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide analog of ACTH(4-10) that powerfully upregulates BDNF, NGF, and GDNF expression in hippocampus and cortex through melanocortin receptor activation. Enhances NMDA receptor-mediated neurotransmission, increases dopamine and serotonin metabolite levels, modulates immune function through cytokine regulation, and promotes neurogenesis. Approved in Russia for stroke recovery and cognitive enhancement.
cognitive_domains: memory, attention, neuroplasticity, stroke recovery, learning, stress resilience
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, basal ganglia, cortex-wide, limbic system

primary_targets: AMPA and NMDA receptor modulation; BDNF/NGF upregulation; antioxidant
mechanism_text: Noopept modulates both AMPA and NMDA glutamate receptors, increasing excitatory postsynaptic potentials. Its active metabolite cycloprolylglycine upregulates BDNF and NGF expression in hippocampus. Enhances alpha and beta-wave oscillatory activity, increases cortical and hippocampal neuroplasticity, provides neuroprotection through antioxidant and anti-inflammatory mechanisms. 1000x more potent than piracetam by weight.
cognitive_domains: memory, learning, neuroprotection, attention, anxiety reduction, neuroplasticity
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide, amygdala, entorhinal cortex

primary_targets: AMPA receptor PAM; modulates metabotropic glutamate receptors; weak cholinergic and dopaminergic effects
mechanism_text: Aniracetam acts as a positive allosteric modulator of AMPA receptors, slowing desensitization kinetics and enhancing glutamatergic transmission. It also modulates metabotropic glutamate receptors, increases acetylcholine release in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, and has anxiolytic properties through dopaminergic and serotonergic modulation. Enhances long-term potentiation in hippocampal circuits.
cognitive_domains: verbal fluency, anxiety reduction, memory formation, creativity, social cognition
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, amygdala, temporal cortex

primary_targets: AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulator; weak effect on NMDA, acetylcholine
mechanism_text: Piracetam enhances AMPA receptor-mediated glutamatergic neurotransmission through positive allosteric modulation, increases membrane fluidity in neuronal lipid bilayers, modulates acetylcholine turnover in hippocampus and cortex, and restores age-related decreases in muscarinic receptor density. Improves mitochondrial electron transport chain function under hypoxic conditions.
cognitive_domains: verbal fluency, working memory, memory consolidation, learning, cognitive flexibility
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, temporal cortex, Broca's area

primary_targets: DAT and NET inhibitor; increases synaptic dopamine and norepinephrine
mechanism_text: Methylphenidate blocks dopamine and norepinephrine transporters, increasing synaptic concentrations of both catecholamines. Primary cognitive effects mediated by enhanced dopamine in prefrontal cortex (improving executive function) and norepinephrine in locus coeruleus-cortical projections (improving attention). Unlike amphetamine, does not promote vesicular release. Inverted-U dose-response on cognition: low doses improve PFC function, high doses impair it.
cognitive_domains: sustained attention, working memory, executive function, impulse control, ADHD
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, striatum, anterior cingulate, parietal cortex, cerebellum

primary_targets: Adenosine A1 and A2A receptor antagonist; PDE inhibitor; ryanodine receptor modulator
mechanism_text: Caffeine is a non-selective adenosine receptor antagonist, primarily blocking A1 and A2A receptors. A2A antagonism in striatum increases dopaminergic signaling indirectly. A1 antagonism throughout cortex reduces inhibitory tone on excitatory neurotransmission. Also inhibits phosphodiesterases (increasing cAMP) and mobilizes intracellular calcium via ryanodine receptors. Increases norepinephrine, acetylcholine, and serotonin release.
cognitive_domains: alertness, sustained attention, reaction time, vigilance, psychomotor speed
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, anterior cingulate, striatum, thalamus, cortex-wide

primary_targets: Nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) agonist, especially alpha4-beta2 and alpha7 subtypes
mechanism_text: Nicotine is a potent agonist at nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, with highest affinity for alpha4-beta2 subtype (involved in reward and attention) and alpha7 subtype (involved in cognition and sensory gating). Stimulates dopamine release in nucleus accumbens and prefrontal cortex, enhances glutamate release, increases norepinephrine and acetylcholine. Improves sustained attention, working memory, and sensory gating. Rapid desensitization leads to tolerance.
cognitive_domains: sustained attention, working memory, sensory gating, reaction time, episodic memory
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, ventral tegmental area, nucleus accumbens, hippocampus, thalamus

primary_targets: Selective, reversible acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; sigma-1 receptor agonist
mechanism_text: Donepezil is a selective, reversible inhibitor of acetylcholinesterase with high brain penetration and long half-life (70 hours). Increases synaptic acetylcholine throughout cortex and hippocampus. Also agonizes sigma-1 receptors, potentially providing additional neuroprotective effects. FDA-approved for all stages of Alzheimer's disease. Enhances P300 event-related potential amplitude, reflecting improved attentional resource allocation. Most prescribed cholinesterase inhibitor worldwide.
cognitive_domains: Alzheimer's disease, attention, memory, global cognition, vascular dementia
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide cholinergic, basal forebrain, temporal cortex, parietal cortex

primary_targets: Tyrosine hydroxylase upregulation; dopamine synthesis enhancement; anxiolytic via GABA modulation; sigma receptor activity
mechanism_text: Bromantane is an adamantane derivative that uniquely upregulates tyrosine hydroxylase and aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase gene expression, increasing endogenous dopamine synthesis rather than blocking reuptake or promoting release. This produces a non-depleting stimulant effect with no tolerance or dependence. Also modulates GABAergic neurotransmission providing anxiolytic effects. Has sigma-1 receptor activity. Approved in Russia as an 'actoprotector' for asthenia and fatigue.
cognitive_domains: motivation, fatigue resistance, anxiolysis, physical performance, asthenia, dopamine synthesis
brain_regions_expected: ventral tegmental area, substantia nigra, striatum, prefrontal cortex, hypothalamus

primary_targets: Nitric oxide modulation; NMDA receptor modulation; GABA-A modulation; cortisol regulation
mechanism_text: Panax ginseng ginsenosides modulate multiple neurotransmitter systems: Rg1 enhances glutamatergic transmission and hippocampal LTP via NMDA receptors, Rb1 provides neuroprotection through GABA-A receptor modulation, and Rg3 modulates voltage-gated ion channels. Increases nitric oxide production enhancing cerebral blood flow. Regulates HPA axis cortisol response. Promotes hippocampal BDNF expression and neurogenesis.
cognitive_domains: working memory, reaction time, mental fatigue, mood, psychomotor performance
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, hypothalamus, anterior cingulate, striatum

aliases: Golden root, Arctic root, rosavin, salidroside
primary_targets: MAO-A/B inhibition; 5-HT modulation; dopamine modulation; cortisol reduction
mechanism_text: Rhodiola rosea active compounds salidroside and rosavins inhibit monoamine oxidases A and B, increasing synaptic availability of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Modulates HPA axis to reduce cortisol secretion under stress. Enhances AMPK-mediated cellular energy production. Increases beta-endorphin levels and heat shock protein expression. Promotes neurogenesis through BDNF pathway activation.
cognitive_domains: fatigue resistance, stress resilience, attention under fatigue, mood, physical endurance
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, locus coeruleus, hypothalamus, hippocampus, raphe nuclei

primary_targets: PAF antagonist; PDE4/5 inhibitor; antioxidant; vasodilator; MAO inhibitor
mechanism_text: Ginkgo biloba extract EGb 761 antagonizes platelet-activating factor, inhibits phosphodiesterases 4 and 5, acts as a mild MAO-A/B inhibitor increasing monoamine neurotransmitter levels, enhances cerebral blood flow through nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation, and provides neuroprotection through potent antioxidant activity against reactive oxygen species. Bilobalide protects mitochondrial function.
cognitive_domains: cerebral blood flow, memory in elderly, attention, visual processing, tinnitus
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, temporal cortex, hippocampus, visual cortex, auditory cortex

primary_targets: Dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine precursor (via tyrosine hydroxylase → DOPA → dopamine)
mechanism_text: L-Tyrosine is the rate-limited precursor for catecholamine synthesis via the tyrosine hydroxylase → L-DOPA → dopamine → norepinephrine → epinephrine pathway. Supplementation replenishes catecholamine stores depleted by stress, sleep deprivation, cold exposure, or multitasking cognitive demand. Effects are most pronounced when catecholamine systems are under high demand, with minimal effects at baseline. Does not increase peak dopamine above normal but prevents depletion-related cognitive decline.
cognitive_domains: stress resilience, working memory under stress, multitasking, cold stress cognition, catecholamine replenishment
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, locus coeruleus, ventral tegmental area, substantia nigra, hypothalamus

primary_targets: Glutathione precursor; glutamate modulation via cystine-glutamate antiporter; anti-inflammatory
mechanism_text: NAC is a precursor to glutathione, the brain's primary endogenous antioxidant. Modulates glutamate homeostasis through the cystine-glutamate antiporter (system Xc-), which exchanges extracellular cystine for intracellular glutamate. This regulates extrasynaptic glutamate tone and prevents excitotoxicity. Reduces neuroinflammation through NF-kB inhibition. Used clinically for OCD, addiction, bipolar, and trichotillomania.
cognitive_domains: compulsive behavior, addiction, glutamate regulation, neuroprotection, impulse control
brain_regions_expected: nucleus accumbens, prefrontal cortex, anterior cingulate, striatum, hippocampus

primary_targets: Mitochondrial fatty acid transport; acetylcholine precursor; mGluR modulation; epigenetic modulation
mechanism_text: ALCAR donates its acetyl group for acetylcholine synthesis and enhances mitochondrial fatty acid beta-oxidation, improving neuronal energy metabolism. Modulates metabotropic glutamate receptors (mGluR2), reduces C-reactive protein and TNF-alpha neuroinflammation, enhances BDNF and NGF signaling, and provides epigenetic modulation through histone acetylation. Used clinically for diabetic neuropathy and age-related cognitive decline.
cognitive_domains: mental energy, age-related cognitive decline, neuropathy, depression, fatigue
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, cerebellum, brainstem, peripheral nerves

primary_targets: AMPA receptor PAM (1000x piracetam potency); CaMKII and PKC activation; glycine binding site modulator
mechanism_text: Sunifiram is a piperazine-derived ampakine that potentiates AMPA receptor currents at nanomolar concentrations, approximately 1000-fold more potent than piracetam. Activates CaMKII and PKC signaling cascades critical for long-term potentiation. Also enhances glycine binding at NMDA receptors, facilitating glutamatergic transmission. Restores scopolamine-induced amnesia in animal models at microgram doses. Not a racetam despite functional similarity.
cognitive_domains: memory formation, LTP enhancement, learning, attention, amnesia reversal
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, entorhinal cortex, cortex-wide glutamatergic

primary_targets: Mu and delta opioid receptor agonist; glutamate modulation; AMPA receptor trafficking; BDNF enhancer
mechanism_text: Tianeptine is an atypical antidepressant that acts primarily as a mu and delta opioid receptor agonist, modulates AMPA receptor trafficking and glutamatergic plasticity in hippocampus, normalizes stress-induced dendritic remodeling in hippocampus and amygdala, and enhances BDNF expression. Previously misclassified as a serotonin reuptake enhancer. Reverses stress-induced hippocampal atrophy and restores LTP. Approved in France, not in USA.
cognitive_domains: depression, stress resilience, hippocampal plasticity, anxiety, cognitive symptoms of depression
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, amygdala, prefrontal cortex, nucleus accumbens, habenula

aliases: Cavinton, ethyl apovincaminate, Vinca minor derivative
primary_targets: PDE1 inhibitor; voltage-gated sodium channel blocker; cerebral vasodilator; anti-inflammatory (NF-kB inhibition)
mechanism_text: Vinpocetine selectively inhibits phosphodiesterase type 1 (PDE1), increasing cAMP and cGMP in cerebral vasculature and neurons. Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels providing neuroprotection against excitotoxicity. Enhances cerebral blood flow by 7-12% selectively without affecting systemic blood pressure. Inhibits IKK/NF-kB inflammatory cascade. Enhances glucose and oxygen utilization in brain tissue. Used extensively in Europe and Japan for cerebrovascular insufficiency.
cognitive_domains: cerebral blood flow, memory, stroke recovery, neuroprotection, tinnitus, visual function
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (vascular), hippocampus, temporal cortex, visual cortex, auditory cortex

primary_targets: DMAE ester (acetylcholine precursor); lipofuscin removal; membrane phospholipid turnover enhancer
mechanism_text: Centrophenoxine is an ester of DMAE and pCPA that enhances brain acetylcholine synthesis by providing DMAE as a choline precursor. Uniquely reduces age-related lipofuscin accumulation in neurons, improves neuronal membrane phospholipid turnover, increases RNA and protein synthesis in aging brain cells, and enhances glucose and oxygen uptake in cortical neurons. Has established clinical use in Europe for age-related cognitive decline and senile dementia.
cognitive_domains: age-related cognitive decline, memory in elderly, lipofuscin clearance, neuroprotection
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (membrane effects), hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, basal forebrain

primary_targets: Reversible acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; nicotinic acetylcholine receptor allosteric potentiator (alpha7, alpha4-beta2)
mechanism_text: Galantamine has a unique dual mechanism: reversible acetylcholinesterase inhibition plus allosteric potentiation of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (particularly alpha7 and alpha4-beta2 subtypes). The nicotinic modulation enhances presynaptic glutamate and acetylcholine release beyond what AChE inhibition alone achieves. Improves lucid dreaming when taken during REM sleep periods. FDA-approved for mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's. Originally isolated from snowdrop (Galanthus) and red spider lily.
cognitive_domains: Alzheimer's disease, memory, attention, lucid dreaming, cholinergic-nicotinic synergy
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, basal forebrain, cortex-wide, prefrontal cortex, temporal cortex

primary_targets: Neuronal membrane component; anti-inflammatory (resolvins, neuroprotectin D1); BDNF modulation
mechanism_text: DHA constitutes ~40% of polyunsaturated fatty acids in brain phospholipids, critical for neuronal membrane fluidity, receptor trafficking, and synaptic vesicle dynamics. Metabolized to neuroprotectin D1 and resolvins which resolve neuroinflammation. Enhances BDNF signaling, promotes hippocampal neurogenesis, supports myelination, and modulates dopamine and serotonin systems. Deficiency associated with cognitive impairment, depression, and ADHD.
cognitive_domains: membrane integrity, anti-inflammation, mood, visual processing, infant neurodevelopment
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (structural), hippocampus, retina, prefrontal cortex, white matter

primary_targets: Prodrug of modafinil (hepatic conversion); DAT inhibition; alpha-1 adrenergic agonism
mechanism_text: Adrafinil is a prodrug metabolized in the liver to modafinil and modafinilic acid. Produces wakefulness-promoting effects through the same mechanisms as modafinil: dopamine transporter inhibition, orexin system activation, and histaminergic enhancement. Slower onset (45-60min) and longer duration than modafinil due to hepatic conversion requirement. May produce hepatotoxicity with chronic use due to metabolic intermediates.
cognitive_domains: wakefulness, alertness, attention, vigilance, fatigue resistance
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, lateral hypothalamus, thalamus, anterior cingulate, basal ganglia

primary_targets: GABA-B receptor agonist; alpha2-delta voltage-gated calcium channel blocker; weak GABA-A activity
mechanism_text: Phenibut is a GABA analog that crosses the blood-brain barrier due to its phenyl ring modification. Primarily acts as GABA-B receptor agonist (similar to baclofen) and blocks alpha2-delta subunit of voltage-gated calcium channels (similar to gabapentin). Weak GABA-A activity. Reduces anxiety, improves sleep architecture, and has nootropic effects at low doses through GABAergic modulation. High addiction and dependence potential at recreational doses.
cognitive_domains: anxiety reduction, social cognition, sleep quality, stress response (use with caution - addiction risk)
brain_regions_expected: amygdala, prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, thalamus, cortex-wide inhibitory

primary_targets: Integrated Stress Response (ISR) inhibitor; eIF2B activator; blocks ATF4 pathway
mechanism_text: ISRIB reactivates eIF2B-mediated protein translation by stabilizing eIF2B decamers, overriding the integrated stress response that suppresses protein synthesis under cellular stress. In aged mice, a brief ISRIB treatment rejuvenated hippocampal memory to youthful levels, enhanced working memory, and improved spatial learning. Effects persisted for weeks after treatment. Restored age-related declines in hippocampal long-term potentiation and synapse number. One of the most striking aging-reversal effects observed in cognitive neuroscience.
cognitive_domains: age-related cognitive decline, spatial memory, working memory, hippocampal rejuvenation, LTP restoration
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide (protein synthesis), dentate gyrus

primary_targets: GABAergic and glutamatergic modulation; BDNF upregulation; antioxidant
mechanism_text: Cortexin is a complex of low-molecular-weight neuropeptides extracted from cerebral cortex of cattle and pigs. Modulates GABAergic and glutamatergic neurotransmission balance, promotes BDNF-mediated neuroplasticity, enhances cortical metabolic activity, reduces excitotoxicity, and has antioxidant properties. Approved in Russia for treatment of craniocerebral trauma, encephalopathy, and cognitive disorders in children. Normalizes EEG bioelectrical activity.
cognitive_domains: cortical metabolism, cognitive rehabilitation, pediatric neurodevelopment, TBI recovery, epilepsy
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide, hippocampus, temporal cortex, frontal cortex

primary_targets: Telomerase activation; pineal gland peptide; melatonin secretion enhancement
mechanism_text: Epitalon is a synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) based on epithalamin, a pineal gland extract. Activates telomerase reverse transcriptase in somatic cells, lengthening telomeres. Stimulates melatonin secretion from the pineal gland, restoring circadian rhythm integrity. Modulates gene expression patterns associated with aging. May enhance antioxidant enzyme expression. Khavinson's research suggests geroprotective effects through epigenetic regulation.
cognitive_domains: circadian rhythm, anti-aging, sleep quality, melatonin regulation, longevity
brain_regions_expected: pineal gland, suprachiasmatic nucleus, hypothalamus

primary_targets: Thymus bioregulator; T-cell differentiation; immune reconstitution; neuroimmune axis modulation
mechanism_text: Thymalin is a polypeptide complex isolated from thymus gland that restores T-cell differentiation and immune function. The active dipeptide Glu-Trp (EW) penetrates cell nuclei and binds DNA, modulating expression of genes involved in immune regulation and aging. In Khavinson's landmark 15-year clinical study, thymalin plus epithalamin treatment reduced mortality by 2-fold in elderly subjects. Modulates neuroimmune interactions through cytokine normalization affecting microglial function and neuroinflammation.
cognitive_domains: immune reconstitution, anti-aging, neuroimmune axis, longevity, inflammation reduction
brain_regions_expected: hypothalamus (neuroimmune), cortex-wide (microglial modulation), thymus-brain axis

primary_targets: Thymic dipeptide; T-cell maturation; immune reconstitution; anti-inflammatory cytokine modulation
mechanism_text: Thymogen (Glu-Trp) is the active dipeptide identified from thymalin extract that restores T-cell maturation and immune function. Approved as a pharmaceutical in Russia for immunodeficiency states. Modulates cytokine balance (reduces TNF-alpha, IL-1beta; increases IL-2, IL-10), promotes T-cell differentiation in thymus, and normalizes CD4/CD8 ratios. Neuroimmune relevance: normalizes the inflammatory cytokine milieu that drives age-related neuroinflammation, microglial priming, and blood-brain barrier dysfunction.
cognitive_domains: immune reconstitution, neuroinflammation reduction, anti-aging immunity, cytokine balance
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (via cytokine normalization), hypothalamus, circumventricular organs

primary_targets: Irreversible MAO-B inhibitor (low dose); MAO-A inhibitor (high dose); increases dopamine, PEA, and catecholamines
mechanism_text: At low doses (1-5mg), selegiline selectively and irreversibly inhibits monoamine oxidase B, increasing synaptic dopamine, phenylethylamine (PEA), and trace amine levels. Enhances catecholaminergic neurotransmission in nigrostriatal and mesocortical pathways. Also upregulates superoxide dismutase and catalase antioxidant enzymes. Metabolized to L-methamphetamine and L-amphetamine at trace levels. Neuroprotective in Parkinson's disease models. Used as cognitive enhancer at sub-clinical doses.
cognitive_domains: motivation, dopamine enhancement, neuroprotection, Parkinson's disease, cognitive aging, mood
brain_regions_expected: substantia nigra, striatum, prefrontal cortex, ventral tegmental area, locus coeruleus

primary_targets: Brain magnesium elevation; NMDA receptor modulation; synaptic density enhancer; NR2B subunit expression
mechanism_text: Magnesium L-threonate uniquely elevates brain magnesium levels (other forms do not effectively cross BBB). Increased brain magnesium enhances NMDA receptor function by relieving voltage-dependent Mg2+ block at appropriate membrane potentials, upregulates NR2B subunit expression in hippocampus increasing NMDA receptor plasticity, increases synaptic density in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, and enhances both short-term and long-term memory through improved synaptic plasticity.
cognitive_domains: memory, learning, synaptic plasticity, sleep quality, anxiety reduction, cognitive aging
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide (magnesium homeostasis)

primary_targets: Mitochondrial Complex I-III alternative electron carrier; NOS inhibitor; MAO-A inhibitor; tau aggregation inhibitor
mechanism_text: Methylene blue at low doses (0.5-4 mg/kg) acts as an alternative electron carrier in the mitochondrial respiratory chain, accepting electrons from NADH and transferring them to cytochrome c, bypassing Complexes I-III when they are impaired. Inhibits nitric oxide synthase reducing oxidative stress, inhibits MAO-A increasing serotonin and norepinephrine, and directly inhibits tau protein aggregation. Enhances memory consolidation and extinction learning. Increases brain cytochrome oxidase activity by 25-30%.
cognitive_domains: memory consolidation, fear extinction, mitochondrial energy, Alzheimer's tau pathology, neuroprotection
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, amygdala (extinction), cortex-wide (mitochondrial)

primary_targets: Non-competitive NMDA receptor antagonist (low-affinity, voltage-dependent); 5-HT3 antagonist; nAChR antagonist; D2 agonist
mechanism_text: Memantine is a low-affinity, non-competitive NMDA receptor antagonist that preferentially blocks tonically activated (extrasynaptic) NMDA receptors while preserving normal phasic synaptic transmission. This reduces glutamate excitotoxicity without impairing learning-related NMDA signaling. Also antagonizes 5-HT3 receptors and nicotinic receptors, and has weak D2 agonist activity. FDA-approved for moderate-to-severe Alzheimer's disease. Used off-label for neuroprotection.
cognitive_domains: Alzheimer's disease, neuroprotection, excitotoxicity prevention, cognitive decline
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide, basal ganglia, entorhinal cortex, temporal cortex

primary_targets: Hippocampal neurogenesis stimulator; unknown primary receptor target; BDNF increase
mechanism_text: NSI-189 is a small molecule that stimulates neurogenesis in human hippocampal neural stem cells. Its exact molecular target remains unknown, but it increases hippocampal volume in MDD patients (measurable on MRI), upregulates BDNF, and enhances synaptic plasticity markers. Phase 2 clinical trial in major depression showed cognitive benefits (improved memory, concentration) even in non-responders on mood measures.
cognitive_domains: hippocampal neurogenesis, depression, memory, cognitive symptoms of MDD
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus (dentate gyrus), subventricular zone, entorhinal cortex

primary_targets: Oxytocin receptor agonist; vasopressin V1a receptor partial agonist; GABAergic modulation in amygdala
mechanism_text: Intranasal oxytocin acts on oxytocin receptors densely expressed in amygdala, hippocampus, nucleus accumbens, and prefrontal cortex. Reduces amygdala reactivity to threat stimuli, enhances social cognition and face recognition, modulates GABAergic inhibition in central amygdala (anxiolytic), enhances trust and prosocial behavior, and improves social memory through hippocampal oxytocin receptor activation. Effects are context-dependent and modulated by baseline social functioning. Studied for autism, social anxiety, and PTSD.
cognitive_domains: social cognition, face recognition, trust, anxiety reduction, PTSD, autism social deficits
brain_regions_expected: amygdala, hippocampus, nucleus accumbens, prefrontal cortex, fusiform face area, insula

primary_targets: TrkB receptor agonist (BDNF mimetic); small molecule neurotrophin signaling activator
mechanism_text: 7,8-DHF is a naturally occurring flavonoid that selectively activates TrkB receptors, mimicking BDNF signaling without requiring the BDNF protein. Crosses the blood-brain barrier and promotes downstream Ras-MAPK, PI3K-Akt, and PLCgamma cascades. Enhances hippocampal neurogenesis, synaptogenesis, and dendritic spine formation. Reverses cognitive deficits in Alzheimer's, depression, and PTSD animal models. Represents a breakthrough in small-molecule neurotrophin therapeutics.
cognitive_domains: neuroplasticity, memory, depression, PTSD, neurodegeneration, hippocampal neurogenesis
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, amygdala, cortex-wide (TrkB expressing regions)

primary_targets: Phosphatidylcholine synthesis (Kennedy pathway); CDP-choline precursor; dopamine modulation; synaptogenesis
mechanism_text: Uridine is a pyrimidine nucleoside essential for the Kennedy pathway of phosphatidylcholine synthesis in neuronal membranes. Enhances neurite outgrowth and synaptogenesis, increases striatal dopamine release, promotes dendritic spine formation in hippocampus when combined with DHA and choline. Crosses the blood-brain barrier and is converted to UTP and CTP for RNA and phospholipid synthesis. Often stacked with omega-3 DHA and choline ('Mr. Happy Stack').
cognitive_domains: synaptogenesis, membrane synthesis, mood, dopamine modulation, neuroplasticity
brain_regions_expected: striatum, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide (membrane synthesis)

primary_targets: Microtubule stabilizer; tau interaction modulator; activity-dependent neuroprotective protein (ADNP) fragment
mechanism_text: Davunetide (NAP) is an 8-amino acid neuroprotective peptide derived from activity-dependent neuroprotective protein (ADNP). Binds to tubulin and stabilizes microtubules, protecting axonal transport machinery disrupted in tauopathies. Reduces tau hyperphosphorylation and prevents tau-microtubule dissociation. Intranasal administration achieves brain concentrations. Progressed to Phase 2/3 clinical trials for progressive supranuclear palsy and frontotemporal dementia. Enhances memory and learning in animal models at femtomolar concentrations.
cognitive_domains: tauopathy, microtubule stabilization, neuroprotection, PSP, FTD, memory
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (microtubule), hippocampus, frontal cortex, basal ganglia, brainstem

primary_targets: HGF/c-Met receptor system activation; angiotensin IV analog; synaptic plasticity enhancer
mechanism_text: Dihexa is a modified angiotensin IV analog that activates the hepatocyte growth factor/c-Met receptor system in brain, promoting dendritic spine formation, synaptogenesis, and long-term potentiation at concentrations 10 million times more potent than BDNF. Enhances hippocampal connectivity and spatial memory in animal models. Crosses blood-brain barrier. Proposed mechanism involves augmenting HGF/c-Met dimerization.
cognitive_domains: memory formation, synaptic plasticity, spatial memory, dementia (preclinical)
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, entorhinal cortex, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide synaptogenesis

primary_targets: Enhanced Selank with improved stability; GABAergic; serotonergic; BDNF; enkephalinase inhibition
mechanism_text: NA-Selank is an N-acetylated amidate form of Selank with improved enzymatic stability and enhanced bioavailability compared to native Selank. Retains all mechanisms of Selank (GABAergic enhancement, enkephalinase inhibition, BDNF upregulation, serotonin modulation) with prolonged duration of action. The acetylation protects the N-terminus from aminopeptidase degradation while the C-terminal amidation resists carboxypeptidase cleavage. Preferred form for intranasal administration.
cognitive_domains: anxiety reduction, memory, attention, mood, immune modulation, stress resilience
brain_regions_expected: amygdala, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, raphe nuclei, hypothalamus

primary_targets: Enhanced Semax with improved stability; BDNF/NGF/GDNF upregulation; melanocortin; dopaminergic/serotonergic
mechanism_text: NA-Semax is the N-acetylated amidate form of Semax with improved peptidase resistance and extended half-life. Retains all mechanisms of Semax (powerful BDNF/NGF/GDNF upregulation, melanocortin receptor activation, dopamine and serotonin modulation) while providing more sustained neurotrophin expression. Shows enhanced immunomodulatory properties compared to native Semax, with stronger effects on pro-inflammatory cytokine reduction. The most commonly used form outside of Russian clinical settings.
cognitive_domains: memory, neuroplasticity, attention, stroke recovery, learning, immune modulation, stress resilience
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide, basal ganglia, limbic system

primary_targets: CNTF receptor pathway activation; BDNF upregulation; neurogenesis promoter; anti-inflammatory
mechanism_text: P21 is a synthetic tetrapeptide derived from the active region of ciliary neurotrophic factor (CNTF), identified as the minimal neurogenic fragment of cerebrolysin. Activates CNTF receptor complex triggering JAK-STAT signaling cascade, upregulates BDNF expression, promotes adult hippocampal neurogenesis in dentate gyrus, reduces neuroinflammation, and decreases tau hyperphosphorylation. Crosses blood-brain barrier when administered intranasally. Rescues cognitive deficits and synaptic loss in Alzheimer's mouse models.
cognitive_domains: neurogenesis, Alzheimer's disease, memory, synaptic rescue, tau reduction, cognitive decline
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus (dentate gyrus), subventricular zone, prefrontal cortex, entorhinal cortex

primary_targets: GABAergic enhancement; enkephalinase inhibition; serotonergic modulation; BDNF upregulation
mechanism_text: Selank is a synthetic peptide analog of tuftsin (immunoglobulin fragment) that enhances GABAergic neurotransmission, inhibits enkephalin-degrading enzymes increasing endogenous opioid peptide levels, modulates serotonin and norepinephrine metabolism, and upregulates BDNF expression. Has anxiolytic effects comparable to benzodiazepines without sedation or dependence. Modulates IL-6 and affects gene expression of 36 genes involved in GABAergic signaling.
cognitive_domains: anxiety reduction, memory, attention, immune modulation, stress resilience
brain_regions_expected: amygdala, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, hypothalamus, raphe nuclei

primary_targets: Neurotrophic factor mimetic (BDNF, GDNF, CNTF-like); calpain inhibition; anti-apoptotic
mechanism_text: Cerebrolysin is a mixture of low-molecular-weight neuropeptides and amino acids derived from porcine brain tissue that mimics the action of endogenous neurotrophic factors. Activates PI3K/Akt survival signaling, inhibits calpain-mediated neurodegeneration, promotes neurogenesis and synaptogenesis, reduces amyloid-beta aggregation, and enhances cholinergic neurotransmission. FDA-designated orphan drug for TBI. Used clinically in Europe and Asia for stroke and dementia.
cognitive_domains: stroke recovery, traumatic brain injury, dementia, neuroplasticity, memory
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide, basal ganglia, cerebellum, white matter

primary_targets: Cell membrane phospholipid component; cortisol reduction; acetylcholine and dopamine modulation
mechanism_text: Phosphatidylserine is a phospholipid component of neuronal cell membranes, critical for membrane fluidity, vesicle fusion (neurotransmitter release), and cell signaling. Supplementation enhances acetylcholine and dopamine release, reduces exercise-induced cortisol, activates protein kinase C, and supports phospholipid turnover in aging neurons. Blunts HPA axis cortisol response to stress.
cognitive_domains: memory in aging, attention, cortisol reduction, exercise recovery, cognitive decline
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (membrane component), hippocampus, prefrontal cortex

primary_targets: NMDA receptor antagonist (polyamine site); imidazoline receptor agonist (I1, I2); nNOS inhibitor; nicotinic ACh receptor blocker; alpha-2 adrenergic agonist
mechanism_text: Agmatine is an endogenous polyamine neurotransmitter derived from arginine decarboxylation. Blocks NMDA receptors at the polyamine binding site, providing neuroprotection without dissociative effects. Agonizes imidazoline I1 receptors (lowering blood pressure) and I2 receptors (modulating monoamine metabolism). Inhibits neuronal nitric oxide synthase. Blocks nicotinic acetylcholine receptors. Potentiates opioid analgesia while blocking tolerance. Has antidepressant, anxiolytic, and neuroprotective properties.
cognitive_domains: neuroprotection, depression, pain modulation, opioid tolerance prevention, anxiety, excitotoxicity
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, hypothalamus, prefrontal cortex, periaqueductal gray, locus coeruleus, cortex-wide

primary_targets: NF-kB inhibitor; anti-inflammatory; BDNF upregulation; amyloid-beta binding; MAO-B inhibition
mechanism_text: Curcumin inhibits NF-kB-mediated neuroinflammation, binds and disaggregates amyloid-beta plaques, inhibits MAO-B enzyme increasing dopamine availability, upregulates BDNF expression, chelates heavy metals, enhances DHA synthesis in brain, and modulates multiple kinase pathways (mTOR, GSK-3beta). Bioavailability is poor unless formulated with piperine or lipid nanoparticles. Theracurmin and Longvida forms achieve brain-relevant concentrations.
cognitive_domains: anti-inflammatory neuroprotection, memory in aging, depression, amyloid clearance, mood
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, amygdala, cortex-wide (anti-inflammatory)

primary_targets: Metabotropic glutamate receptor (mGluR) modulator; GABA-B receptor upregulation; cholinergic enhancement
mechanism_text: Fasoracetam uniquely modulates Group III metabotropic glutamate receptors (mGluR4, mGluR7, mGluR8) and upregulates GABA-B receptor expression, distinguishing it from other racetams. Enhances high-affinity choline uptake in hippocampus. Currently in Phase 2 clinical trials for ADHD in adolescents with mGluR gene mutations (15q11.2 deletions). Restores GABA-B receptor density depleted by chronic baclofen exposure.
cognitive_domains: ADHD, anxiety, memory, GABA-B upregulation, glutamate modulation
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, basal ganglia, cortex-wide GABAergic

primary_targets: NMDA receptor glycine site modulator; nicotinic ACh receptor potentiation; PKC and CaMKII activation; GABAergic modulation
mechanism_text: Nefiracetam has a uniquely broad receptor profile among racetams: potentiates NMDA receptor activity through the glycine binding site, enhances nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (alpha4-beta2) currents through direct binding, activates PKC and CaMKII signaling, and modulates GABA-A receptor Cl- currents. Opens L-type calcium channels in hippocampal neurons. Enhances both cholinergic and glutamatergic long-term potentiation. Studied for post-stroke apathy and cognitive recovery.
cognitive_domains: apathy, post-stroke cognition, memory consolidation, learning, attention
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, basal forebrain, striatum, temporal cortex

primary_targets: AMPA and NMDA receptor modulator; enhances cholinergic neurotransmission
mechanism_text: Oxiracetam enhances both AMPA and NMDA receptor-mediated glutamatergic neurotransmission, increases hippocampal acetylcholine release, stimulates protein kinase C activity, and promotes long-term potentiation. More potent than piracetam on memory consolidation metrics. Increases D-aspartate release in hippocampus.
cognitive_domains: logical reasoning, memory consolidation, spatial memory, attention, learning speed
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, entorhinal cortex, prefrontal cortex, parietal cortex

primary_targets: AMPA PAM; dopamine reuptake inhibition (DAT); nicotinic acetylcholine receptor modulation
mechanism_text: Phenylpiracetam combines AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulation with dopamine reuptake inhibition via DAT binding, and modulation of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors. The phenyl group adds dopaminergic and stimulant properties absent in piracetam. Increases locomotor activity and cold stress tolerance. Enhances cortical excitability.
cognitive_domains: psychomotor speed, motivation, attention, physical endurance, working memory, cold tolerance
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, striatum, motor cortex, hippocampus, anterior cingulate

primary_targets: High-affinity choline uptake enhancer; indirect cholinergic potentiation
mechanism_text: Pramiracetam increases high-affinity choline uptake in hippocampus, thereby enhancing acetylcholine synthesis and release. Has minimal direct effect on glutamate or GABA receptors. Increases nitric oxide synthase activity in cerebral cortex. Most potent racetam for episodic memory enhancement per milligram basis.
cognitive_domains: episodic memory, long-term memory formation, memory retrieval, object recognition
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, medial temporal lobe, basal forebrain, prefrontal cortex

aliases: Yuan Zhi, tenuifolin, onjisaponin B, BT-11
primary_targets: BACE1 inhibitor (amyloid pathway); acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; NMDA receptor modulation; expectorant saponins
mechanism_text: Polygala tenuifolia root extract contains tenuigenin and tenuifolin which inhibit BACE1 (beta-secretase), reducing amyloid-beta production. Onjisaponin B inhibits acetylcholinesterase and promotes nerve growth factor secretion from astrocytes. Tenuigenin modulates NMDA receptor activity and enhances hippocampal long-term potentiation. Used in Traditional Chinese Medicine for 2000+ years as a cognition-enhancing and memory-preserving herb. BT-11 extract in clinical trials for mild cognitive impairment.
cognitive_domains: memory, Alzheimer's disease prevention, NGF secretion, learning, anti-amnesic
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, basal forebrain, temporal cortex, entorhinal cortex

primary_targets: Thiamine pyrophosphate precursor; dopaminergic modulation; glutamatergic modulation
mechanism_text: Sulbutiamine is a lipophilic thiamine (B1) derivative that crosses the blood-brain barrier more efficiently than thiamine. Increases thiamine pyrophosphate levels in brain, upregulates D1 dopamine receptor density in prefrontal cortex, modulates glutamatergic transmission in hippocampus, and reduces cholinergic transmission in specific circuits. Used for asthenia and psychogenic erectile dysfunction in France.
cognitive_domains: mental fatigue, motivation, asthenia, memory, psychomotor speed
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, striatum, reticular activating system

primary_targets: GABA-A receptor agonist; glycine receptor agonist; osmoregulator; antioxidant
mechanism_text: Taurine is an inhibitory amino acid that activates GABA-A and glycine receptors, modulates calcium homeostasis through voltage-gated calcium channel regulation, acts as an intracellular osmoregulator maintaining cell volume, and provides antioxidant neuroprotection. Enhances GABAergic inhibition in hippocampus and cortex. Involved in neuronal development and synaptogenesis during critical periods.
cognitive_domains: anxiety reduction, neuroprotection, GABAergic modulation, sleep, neural development
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cerebellum, cortex-wide, retina, hypothalamus

primary_targets: AMPA receptor PAM; structurally related to sunifiram; CaMKII activation
mechanism_text: Unifiram is a structural analog of sunifiram with similar AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulation activity. Enhances hippocampal long-term potentiation through CaMKII-dependent mechanisms. Reverses scopolamine-induced and mecamylamine-induced amnesia in passive avoidance paradigms. Less studied than sunifiram but appears equipotent in anti-amnesic models.
cognitive_domains: memory formation, anti-amnesic, learning, LTP
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, entorhinal cortex

primary_targets: AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulator; designed for treatment-resistant depression
mechanism_text: TAK-653 is a clinical-stage AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulator being developed for treatment-resistant depression. Enhances AMPA receptor-mediated fast excitatory neurotransmission with improved selectivity over earlier ampakines. Designed to enhance synaptic plasticity and BDNF-TrkB signaling without the seizure risk of non-selective glutamate agonists. Completed Phase 2 trials. Represents next-generation glutamatergic approach to cognitive and mood enhancement.
cognitive_domains: treatment-resistant depression, synaptic plasticity, BDNF signaling, antidepressant
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, anterior cingulate, cortex-wide glutamatergic

primary_targets: Catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) inhibitor; increases prefrontal dopamine
mechanism_text: Tolcapone is a potent, reversible inhibitor of catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT), the primary enzyme degrading dopamine in prefrontal cortex where dopamine transporter expression is low. Increases synaptic dopamine specifically in PFC without significantly affecting striatal dopamine. COMT Val158Met polymorphism determines individual response — Val/Val carriers (high COMT activity, low baseline PFC dopamine) benefit most. FDA-approved for Parkinson's. Studied as a cognitive enhancer for executive function deficits.
cognitive_domains: executive function, prefrontal dopamine, working memory, cognitive flexibility, Parkinson's
brain_regions_expected: prefrontal cortex, anterior cingulate, dorsolateral PFC, orbitofrontal cortex

primary_targets: Alpha-1 adrenergic antagonist; PDE inhibitor; dopamine agonist (D1/D2); serotonin agonist; cerebral vasodilator
mechanism_text: Nicergoline is an ergot alkaloid derivative that antagonizes alpha-1 adrenergic receptors increasing cerebral blood flow, inhibits phosphodiesterases enhancing cAMP/cGMP signaling, acts as a partial dopamine D1/D2 agonist, modulates serotonergic neurotransmission, and enhances cholinergic function through acetylcholine release promotion. Increases cerebral glucose utilization and ATP production. Widely prescribed in Europe and Asia for cerebrovascular insufficiency, vertigo, and mild-moderate dementia.
cognitive_domains: cerebral blood flow, dementia, vertigo, age-related cognitive decline, visual function
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (vascular), prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, visual cortex, cerebellum

aliases: Oroxylum indicum bark extract, oroxylin A, chrysin, baicalein
primary_targets: Dopamine reuptake inhibitor; GABA-A receptor modulator (benzodiazepine site); PDE4 inhibitor
mechanism_text: Sabroxy standardized extract from Oroxylum indicum contains oroxylin A which inhibits dopamine reuptake through DAT binding, providing mild stimulant and motivational effects. Chrysin and baicalein modulate GABA-A receptors at the benzodiazepine binding site, providing anxiolytic effects without full sedation. Oroxylin A also inhibits PDE4, increasing cAMP in neurons, and exhibits neuroprotective anti-inflammatory activity. The combination produces a unique stimulant-plus-anxiolytic profile.
cognitive_domains: motivation, focus, anxiolysis, dopamine enhancement, neuroprotection
brain_regions_expected: striatum, prefrontal cortex, nucleus accumbens, amygdala, ventral tegmental area

primary_targets: Bronchial epithelium bioregulator; respiratory mucosa gene expression; anti-inflammatory
mechanism_text: Bronchogen (Ala-Glu-Asp) is a synthetic tripeptide bioregulator targeting bronchial epithelial cell gene expression. Restores normal mucosa function and reduces chronic airway inflammation. Brain relevance is indirect through improved oxygenation and reduced systemic inflammatory burden affecting neuroinflammation. Part of Khavinson's comprehensive organ-specific peptide bioregulation system where each short peptide targets specific tissue gene expression patterns.
cognitive_domains: respiratory function, oxygenation, systemic inflammation reduction (indirect brain effects)
brain_regions_expected: indirect effects via improved oxygenation and reduced systemic inflammation

primary_targets: Cardiac myocyte bioregulator; cardiovascular gene expression; cardioprotective
mechanism_text: Cardiogen (Ala-Glu-Asp-Arg) is a synthetic tetrapeptide bioregulator targeting cardiac myocyte gene expression. Restores cardiomyocyte function in aging hearts, modulates genes involved in contractility and calcium handling. Brain relevance through cardiovascular-cerebrovascular coupling: cardiac output directly determines cerebral blood flow. Improved cardiac function enhances brain perfusion. Khavinson's research demonstrates that this peptide penetrates cell nuclei and specifically interacts with cardiac gene promoter regions.
cognitive_domains: cardiovascular health, cerebral perfusion (indirect), cardioprotection, aging
brain_regions_expected: cerebral vasculature (indirect via cardiac output), cortex-wide perfusion

primary_targets: Respiratory/bronchial mucosa bioregulator; mucosal gene expression normalization
mechanism_text: Chonluten (Glu-Asp-Gly) is a tripeptide bioregulator for bronchopulmonary system function. Normalizes gene expression in respiratory epithelial cells. Like other Khavinson peptides, penetrates cell membranes and interacts with DNA, modulating transcription of tissue-specific genes. Cognitive relevance is indirect through optimized respiratory gas exchange affecting brain oxygen supply. Part of the systematic organ-specific bioregulation approach.
cognitive_domains: respiratory optimization, oxygenation (indirect cognitive effects)
brain_regions_expected: indirect effects via respiratory optimization

primary_targets: Immune system bioregulator; T-lymphocyte and NK cell modulation; immune gene expression
mechanism_text: Crystagen (Glu-Asp-Pro) is a tripeptide bioregulator targeting immune system gene expression, particularly modulating T-lymphocyte differentiation and natural killer cell activity. Normalizes immune function by restoring age-related decline in immune cell gene expression patterns. Relevant to brain function through neuroimmune interactions: normalized peripheral immune function reduces chronic neuroinflammation, microglial activation, and blood-brain barrier inflammatory signaling.
cognitive_domains: immune modulation, neuroimmune axis, anti-inflammatory (indirect neuroprotection)
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (via reduced neuroinflammation), hypothalamus (neuroimmune)

primary_targets: Hepatic bioregulator; liver gene expression normalization; chromatin decondensation activator
mechanism_text: Livagen (Lys-Glu-Asp-Ala) is a tetrapeptide bioregulator targeting hepatocyte gene expression. Khavinson's research demonstrates it induces chromatin decondensation in hepatocyte heterochromatin, reactivating silenced gene regions. This epigenetic mechanism is central to Khavinson's theory of peptide bioregulation. Hepatic function normalization indirectly affects brain through improved detoxification, hepatic encephalopathy prevention, and proper protein synthesis for blood-brain barrier maintenance.
cognitive_domains: hepatic function, detoxification, epigenetic gene reactivation, chromatin remodeling
brain_regions_expected: indirect effects via hepatic function and detoxification

primary_targets: Retinal bioregulator; retinal pigment epithelium gene expression; visual pathway neuroprotection
mechanism_text: Normoftal (Lys-Glu-Asp-Pro) is a tetrapeptide targeting retinal tissue gene expression, particularly retinal pigment epithelium and photoreceptor cells. Prevents age-related retinal degeneration by normalizing expression of visual cycle genes and antioxidant enzymes in retina. Brain relevance through visual pathway: retina is developmental brain tissue, and retinal health directly affects visual cortex input. Retinal degeneration correlates with cognitive decline in aging, suggesting shared neurodegenerative mechanisms.
cognitive_domains: retinal neuroprotection, visual function, visual pathway integrity, macular degeneration
brain_regions_expected: retina, lateral geniculate nucleus, visual cortex, optic nerve

primary_targets: Hepatic/GI bioregulator; hepatocyte gene expression; digestive system normalization
mechanism_text: Ovagen (Glu-Asp-Leu) is a tripeptide bioregulator targeting liver and gastrointestinal tract gene expression. Normalizes hepatocyte function and improves digestive enzymatic activity. Indirect brain relevance through gut-brain axis: improved GI function enhances nutrient absorption (B vitamins, amino acid precursors, minerals critical for neurotransmitter synthesis) and modulates gut microbiome-brain signaling. Hepatic normalization improves clearance of neurotoxic metabolites.
cognitive_domains: gut-brain axis, nutrient absorption, hepatic function, digestive optimization
brain_regions_expected: indirect effects via gut-brain axis and improved nutrient delivery

primary_targets: Pancreatic bioregulator; insulin-producing beta cell gene expression; glucose metabolism normalization
mechanism_text: Pancragen (Lys-Glu-Asp-Trp) is a tetrapeptide bioregulator targeting pancreatic beta-cell gene expression. Restores insulin secretion capacity in aging pancreas, normalizes glucose metabolism. Brain relevance is significant: insulin signaling in brain affects hippocampal synaptic plasticity, glucose-dependent memory consolidation, and insulin-degrading enzyme (which also degrades amyloid-beta). Insulin resistance is a major risk factor for Alzheimer's disease. Pancragen may indirectly support brain insulin signaling.
cognitive_domains: glucose metabolism, insulin signaling, brain insulin, Alzheimer's risk reduction (indirect)
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus (insulin receptors), hypothalamus, cortex-wide (glucose metabolism)

primary_targets: Pineal gland bioregulator; melatonin modulation; antioxidant gene expression
mechanism_text: Pinealon (Glu-Asp-Arg) is a short peptide bioregulator targeting pineal gland function. Penetrates cell membranes and interacts with DNA, modulating expression of genes involved in oxidative stress response. Enhances melatonin synthesis capacity, regulates circadian-dependent gene expression, and may protect against age-related pineal calcification. Part of Khavinson's peptide bioregulation theory targeting organ-specific gene expression restoration.
cognitive_domains: circadian rhythm, sleep, pineal function, anti-aging
brain_regions_expected: pineal gland, suprachiasmatic nucleus, hypothalamus

aliases: Lys-Glu-Asp-Pro (same sequence as Normoftal, different target)
primary_targets: Pancreatic exocrine bioregulator; digestive enzyme gene expression; GI tract normalization
mechanism_text: Suprefort targets pancreatic exocrine function with the same KEDP sequence as Normoftal, illustrating Khavinson's principle that tissue context determines peptide function. Normalizes pancreatic enzyme secretion and digestive function. Demonstrates the theoretical framework where short peptides serve as informational signals recognized by specific chromatin regions in target tissues. Brain relevance through nutrition-cognition axis: optimized digestion supports brain nutrient supply.
cognitive_domains: digestive function, nutrient absorption, nutrition-cognition axis (indirect)
brain_regions_expected: indirect effects via nutritional optimization

primary_targets: Hepatoprotective tripeptide; liver regeneration gene expression; antioxidant gene activation
mechanism_text: Svetinorm (Lys-Glu-Asp) is a hepatoprotective tripeptide bioregulator that normalizes liver-specific gene expression patterns, promotes hepatocyte regeneration, and activates antioxidant enzyme gene transcription. Enhances liver's capacity for detoxification and protein synthesis. Brain relevance through hepatocerebral axis: liver dysfunction directly impairs brain function through accumulation of neurotoxic substances (ammonia, manganese, aromatic amino acids) and reduced synthesis of brain-essential proteins.
cognitive_domains: hepatoprotection, detoxification, hepatocerebral axis, anti-aging
brain_regions_expected: indirect effects via hepatic detoxification and protein synthesis

primary_targets: Testicular bioregulator; testosterone synthesis modulation; HPG axis peptide
mechanism_text: Testagen (Lys-Glu-Asp-Gly) is a synthetic tetrapeptide bioregulator targeting testicular function and the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis. Restores testosterone synthesis in aged testes by modulating gene expression in Leydig cells. Through testosterone's effects on brain androgen receptors, indirectly modulates hippocampal synaptic plasticity, prefrontal executive function, and amygdala emotional processing. Part of Khavinson's bioregulatory peptide system for age-related hormonal decline.
cognitive_domains: testosterone modulation, HPG axis, mood, motivation, age-related hormonal decline
brain_regions_expected: hypothalamus (GnRH), hippocampus (androgen receptors), prefrontal cortex, amygdala

primary_targets: Urinary system bioregulator; bladder/prostate gene expression; smooth muscle function
mechanism_text: Vesilut (Lys-Glu) targets urinary system gene expression, normalizing bladder and prostate smooth muscle function. Has the same dipeptide sequence as Vilon but is formulated and dosed for urinary tract bioregulation. This illustrates Khavinson's principle that peptide bioregulatory effects depend on tissue context — the same peptide sequence can have different functional outcomes depending on target tissue. Minimal direct brain relevance beyond autonomic nervous system regulation of urinary function.
cognitive_domains: autonomic regulation, urinary function (minimal direct cognitive relevance)
brain_regions_expected: pontine micturition center, autonomic nuclei (indirect)

primary_targets: Thymus bioregulator; immune modulation; epigenetic gene regulation
mechanism_text: Vilon (Lys-Glu) is a synthetic dipeptide bioregulator of thymic function. Modulates T-lymphocyte differentiation and immune system function. Through neuroimmune interactions, affects brain function via cytokine signaling and microglial activity modulation. Part of Khavinson's bioregulatory peptide theory where short peptides restore organ-specific gene expression patterns disrupted by aging.
cognitive_domains: immune modulation, neuroimmune axis, anti-aging, inflammation regulation
brain_regions_expected: hypothalamus (neuroimmune axis), microglia-mediated cortex-wide effects

primary_targets: mTORC1 inhibitor; autophagy activator; immunomodulator; anti-senescent
mechanism_text: Rapamycin inhibits mechanistic target of rapamycin complex 1 (mTORC1), activating autophagy and clearing damaged proteins and organelles from neurons. Intermittent low-dose rapamycin in aged mice restores hippocampal-dependent spatial memory, reduces neuroinflammation by dampening senescent cell SASP, enhances cerebrovascular function, and restores blood-brain barrier integrity. Reduces age-related microglial activation. Currently in human longevity trials (PEARL). Cognitive effects likely mediated through autophagy-dependent clearance of toxic aggregates.
cognitive_domains: cognitive aging, autophagy, neuroprotection, vascular health, senolytic, memory in aging
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide (autophagy/vascular), cerebral vasculature, prefrontal cortex

primary_targets: Mitochondrial biogenesis activator (PGC-1alpha); redox cofactor; NMDA receptor modulation; NGF enhancer
mechanism_text: PQQ activates PGC-1alpha transcription factor driving mitochondrial biogenesis, increasing mitochondrial density in neurons. Acts as a potent redox cofactor (5000x more catalytic cycles than vitamin C), modulates NMDA receptor function as a redox-sensitive site modifier, enhances NGF secretion, and protects against oxidative stress. Synergistic with CoQ10 for mitochondrial energy production.
cognitive_domains: mitochondrial energy, neuroprotection, memory, sleep quality, inflammation reduction
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (mitochondrial), hippocampus, substantia nigra, cerebellum

primary_targets: Coenzyme Q10 analog; electron carrier in mitochondrial complex I bypass; antioxidant
mechanism_text: Idebenone is a synthetic short-chain analog of coenzyme Q10 that acts as an electron carrier in the mitochondrial respiratory chain, capable of bypassing dysfunctional Complex I and donating electrons directly to Complex III. More water-soluble than CoQ10, achieving higher brain concentrations. Provides potent antioxidant protection against lipid peroxidation in neuronal membranes. FDA-approved for Leber's hereditary optic neuropathy. Investigated for Friedreich's ataxia and Alzheimer's disease.
cognitive_domains: mitochondrial energy, neuroprotection, Friedreich's ataxia, cognitive decline, visual function
brain_regions_expected: cortex-wide (mitochondrial), substantia nigra, cerebellum, optic nerve, hippocampus

primary_targets: Orexin receptor 1 and 2 (OX1R, OX2R) agonist; wakefulness-promoting; feeding behavior modulator
mechanism_text: Orexin-A is a 33-amino acid neuropeptide produced by lateral hypothalamic neurons that promotes wakefulness through excitation of monoaminergic and cholinergic arousal centers. Activates OX1R (linked to reward and arousal) and OX2R (linked to sleep-wake regulation). Intranasal orexin-A restores cognitive function after sleep deprivation in primates without generalized arousal. Orexin deficiency causes narcolepsy. Orexin system is a key target for understanding arousal-cognition coupling.
cognitive_domains: wakefulness, arousal, sleep deprivation recovery, reward, feeding, narcolepsy
brain_regions_expected: lateral hypothalamus, locus coeruleus, tuberomammillary nucleus, VTA, prefrontal cortex, dorsal raphe

aliases: Pituitary adenylate cyclase-activating polypeptide, PACAP-38, PACAP-27
primary_targets: PAC1 receptor agonist; VPAC1/VPAC2 agonist; cAMP activation; neuroprotective; anti-inflammatory
mechanism_text: PACAP is a 38-amino acid neuropeptide that potently activates adenylate cyclase through PAC1 receptors in brain. Provides neuroprotection against ischemia, traumatic brain injury, and neurodegeneration through PKA/CREB/BDNF signaling cascade activation. Modulates microglial inflammatory response, promotes oligodendrocyte survival and myelination, enhances hippocampal synaptic plasticity, and regulates circadian rhythm through SCN PAC1 receptors. PACAP deficiency is linked to PTSD vulnerability (genetic association).
cognitive_domains: neuroprotection, PTSD vulnerability, circadian rhythm, memory, brain injury recovery
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, hypothalamus (SCN), cortex-wide, amygdala, cerebellum, brainstem

primary_targets: Nitric oxide system modulation; GABAergic; dopaminergic system modulation; angiogenesis
mechanism_text: BPC-157 is a gastric pentadecapeptide that modulates the nitric oxide system, interacts with the dopaminergic system (counteracts dopamine agonist and antagonist effects), modulates GABA-A receptor signaling, promotes angiogenesis through VEGF upregulation, and enhances peripheral and central nerve repair. Counteracts both haloperidol and amphetamine-induced behavioral changes, suggesting homeostatic dopamine regulation.
cognitive_domains: nerve regeneration, dopamine homeostasis, tissue healing, gut-brain axis
brain_regions_expected: basal ganglia, substantia nigra, hippocampus, peripheral nervous system

primary_targets: Dual STAT3 and ADNP pathway activator; neuroprotective; anti-amyloid
mechanism_text: Colivelin is a 26-amino acid hybrid peptide combining the ADNF-9 fragment, humanin, and a cell-penetrating sequence. Activates both STAT3 signaling (via humanin pathway) and ADNP-dependent neuroprotection simultaneously, providing synergistic protection against amyloid-beta toxicity. More potent than either humanin or ADNF-9 alone at preventing neuronal cell death. Effective at femtomolar concentrations. Prevents memory impairment in Alzheimer's model mice after intracerebroventricular and even intranasal delivery.
cognitive_domains: Alzheimer's neuroprotection, anti-amyloid, memory preservation, dual-pathway activation
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide, entorhinal cortex, basal forebrain

primary_targets: Delta-wave sleep promoter; stress hormone modulator; opioid system modulation; circadian rhythm regulator
mechanism_text: DSIP (Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide) is a nonapeptide that promotes delta-wave (slow-wave) sleep without altering total sleep time, increases sleep spindle density, modulates ACTH and cortisol secretion, interacts with the endogenous opioid system (met-enkephalin and beta-endorphin), and has antioxidant stress-protective properties. Normalizes disturbed sleep patterns and reduces stress-induced damage. Crosses blood-brain barrier. Used in some European clinics for insomnia and chronic pain management.
cognitive_domains: slow-wave sleep, sleep quality, stress reduction, circadian rhythm, cortisol modulation
brain_regions_expected: hypothalamus, thalamus, cortex-wide (sleep oscillations), pineal gland, raphe nuclei

primary_targets: NCAM/FGFR interaction mimetic; FGFR1 activation; synaptogenesis promoter; LTP enhancer
mechanism_text: FGL is a synthetic peptide that mimics the FG loop of the second fibronectin type III domain of neural cell adhesion molecule (NCAM), activating FGFR1 signaling. Promotes neurite outgrowth, synaptogenesis, and presynaptic function. Enhances hippocampal long-term potentiation and improves spatial learning and social memory in aged rats. Reduces neuroinflammation through microglial modulation. Represents a targeted approach to enhancing neuroplasticity through cell adhesion molecule signaling.
cognitive_domains: synaptogenesis, spatial memory, social memory, LTP, neuroplasticity, aging
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cortex-wide (NCAM-expressing regions)

primary_targets: TGF-beta modulator; antioxidant gene activator; anti-inflammatory; stem cell mobilizer; collagen synthesis promoter
mechanism_text: GHK-Cu is a naturally occurring tripeptide-copper complex found in blood plasma that declines with age. Modulates expression of >4,000 genes, upregulating DNA repair genes, antioxidant systems (SOD, glutathione), and stem cell markers while downregulating pro-inflammatory and pro-fibrotic genes. Resets gene expression patterns toward a younger profile. Promotes tissue remodeling through TGF-beta and VEGF modulation. While primarily studied for skin and wound healing, the broad gene expression resetting has implications for brain aging through neurovascular unit maintenance.
cognitive_domains: anti-aging gene expression, tissue remodeling, neurovascular health, inflammation reduction
brain_regions_expected: cerebral vasculature, cortex-wide (gene expression), blood-brain barrier endothelium

primary_targets: IGFBP-3 binding; BAX antagonist; anti-apoptotic; FPRL1/2 receptor agonist; STAT3 activation
mechanism_text: Humanin is a 24-amino acid peptide encoded by mitochondrial DNA that acts as an endogenous neuroprotective factor. Binds IGFBP-3 modulating IGF-1 signaling, directly antagonizes BAX-mediated apoptosis at the mitochondrial membrane, activates STAT3 survival signaling via FPRL1/2 receptors, and reduces amyloid-beta toxicity by binding familial Alzheimer's disease-linked proteins. Circulating humanin levels decline with age. Synthetic analog HNG (S14G substitution) is 1000x more potent.
cognitive_domains: neuroprotection, Alzheimer's, anti-apoptotic, mitochondrial protection, aging
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, cortex-wide (mitochondrial), hypothalamus, occipital cortex

primary_targets: Anti-inflammatory (NF-kB inhibition); melanocortin-related; gut-brain axis modulator
mechanism_text: KPV is the C-terminal tripeptide fragment of alpha-melanocyte stimulating hormone (alpha-MSH) that retains potent anti-inflammatory activity without melanocortin receptor binding. Inhibits NF-kB nuclear translocation and reduces pro-inflammatory cytokine production (IL-1beta, TNF-alpha, IL-6). Modulates gut-brain axis through intestinal immune cell regulation. May contribute to neuroinflammation reduction through systemic anti-inflammatory effects and blood-brain barrier immune signaling.
cognitive_domains: neuroinflammation, gut-brain axis, systemic inflammation, immune modulation
brain_regions_expected: hypothalamus (neuroimmune), cortex-wide (anti-inflammatory), gut-brain axis

primary_targets: G-actin sequestering; anti-inflammatory; angiogenic; stem cell mobilizer; oligodendrocyte differentiation
mechanism_text: Thymosin beta-4 is a 43-amino acid peptide that sequesters G-actin monomers regulating cytoskeletal dynamics, promotes angiogenesis through VEGF pathway activation, mobilizes endogenous stem cells, reduces neuroinflammation via NF-kB suppression, and promotes oligodendrocyte progenitor differentiation and myelination. In brain injury models, enhances neurovascular remodeling and white matter repair. Promotes axonal sprouting from intact neurons to denervated regions after stroke. TB-500 is a synthetic fragment.
cognitive_domains: brain injury recovery, myelination, angiogenesis, stroke recovery, white matter repair
brain_regions_expected: white matter tracts, periventricular zones, cortex-wide (vascular), hippocampus

primary_targets: SIRT1 activator; PDE4 inhibitor; cerebral blood flow enhancer; anti-inflammatory
mechanism_text: Resveratrol activates SIRT1 deacetylase promoting mitochondrial biogenesis and neuroprotection, inhibits PDE4 increasing cAMP in hippocampus, enhances cerebral blood flow through nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation, reduces neuroinflammation via NF-kB suppression, and provides antioxidant protection. May promote hippocampal neurogenesis and improve cerebrovascular function.
cognitive_domains: cerebral blood flow, neuroprotection, memory in aging, cardiovascular-brain axis
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, cerebral vasculature, cortex-wide

primary_targets: High-affinity choline uptake (HACU) enhancer; AMPA PAM
mechanism_text: Coluracetam selectively enhances high-affinity choline uptake in cholinergic terminals, increasing acetylcholine synthesis capacity. Also acts as AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulator. Developed for major depressive disorder with comorbid generalized anxiety. May enhance visual processing and color perception through cholinergic modulation of visual cortex.
cognitive_domains: visual processing, color perception, depression, anxiety, memory formation
brain_regions_expected: visual cortex, hippocampus, basal forebrain, prefrontal cortex

primary_targets: Cholinergic potentiation; possible dopaminergic modulation; mechanism not fully characterized
mechanism_text: PRL-8-53 is a synthetic nootropic that enhances memory acquisition and retention in human studies at a single 5mg dose. The original 1978 study showed 80-200% improvement in word recall, with strongest effects in poor initial performers. Mechanism is not fully elucidated but involves cholinergic potentiation and possible dopamine modulation. Crosses blood-brain barrier rapidly. One of the few nootropics with positive human clinical data despite minimal follow-up research.
cognitive_domains: word recall, memory acquisition, memory retention, verbal memory
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, temporal cortex, Broca's area

primary_targets: GSK-3beta inhibitor; IMPase inhibitor; neuroprotective; increases gray matter volume; BDNF enhancer
mechanism_text: Low-dose lithium (5-20mg elemental vs 300-1200mg clinical) inhibits glycogen synthase kinase 3-beta (GSK-3beta), reducing tau hyperphosphorylation and promoting Wnt/beta-catenin neuroprotective signaling. Inhibits inositol monophosphatase (IMPase) modulating phosphoinositide signaling. Increases gray matter volume in hippocampus and cortex detectable on MRI. Upregulates BDNF, Bcl-2 anti-apoptotic protein, and autophagy pathways. Epidemiological data shows inverse correlation between trace lithium in drinking water and dementia rates.
cognitive_domains: neuroprotection, gray matter preservation, anti-aging, mood stabilization, dementia prevention
brain_regions_expected: hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, anterior cingulate, cortex-wide (gray matter), amygdala
